Title[3071073]: Supplementary Material for Developmental Dilemmas: The Role of Power and Agency
Description[3071079]: Supplementary material providing detailed appendices for the work 'Developmental Dilemmas: The Role of Power and Agency.' The document includes theoretical models, typologies, and game-theoretic frameworks related to political settlements, leader-follower dynamics, the influence of institutional entrepreneurs on social norms, resource conflict, and collective-action problems. It features mathematical models, diagrams, references, and explanatory notes, supporting the main text's exploration of power, agency, and institutional development in economic and political contexts.

Title[3071073]: Developmental Dilemmas: The Role of Power and Agency
Description[3071079]: A scholarly monograph analyzing why developmental policies often fail, focusing on the interplay of power, agency, collective-action problems, and institutional evolution in development economics. Drawing from case studies and theoretical frameworks, the work examines how powerful agents shape institutions, the dynamics of leadership, brokerage, and institutional entrepreneurship, and the emergence of social cleavages and resource conflict. The text synthesizes literature on collective action, new institutionalism, social conflict, political settlements, and identity politics to provide a conceptual foundation for developmental policy analysis.

Title[3071073]: Spaulding Model H Touring Car Announcement
Description[3071079]: Magazine excerpt announcing the Spaulding Model H touring car, previously known as Model G, highlighting its radical body refinements and mechanical changes for 1914. The article details the car's transformation capability into a sleeping car, the addition of an electric reading lamp, increased interior space, streamlined design, and revised fuel tank arrangement. Technical specifications such as the four-cylinder motor, three-point motor suspension, and seating arrangement for five passengers are included. Published by Spaulding Mfg. Title[3071073]: Oral History Interview with Dorothy Lannom on Grinnell During the Depression and World War II
Description[3071079]: A transcript of an oral history interview conducted by Dolores Smith with Dorothy Lannom for the Friends of Stewart Library Oral History Project. The interview covers Dorothy Lannom's experiences in Grinnell, Iowa, during the 1930s and 1940s, including her early life, teaching career, marriage, family life, community involvement, local industry, social clubs, and the impact of the Great Depression and World War II on local society. The conversation also discusses the Lannom family's role in local manufacturing, changes in women's roles, local traditions, and notable community members.

Title[3071073]: Letter from H. R. Ley to Staff Sgt James J. Ley, June 1943
Description[3071079]: Handwritten letter from H. R. Ley in Lakota, Iowa, addressed to Staff Sgt James J. Ley of the 477th Bombardment Squad at Barksdale Field, Louisiana. The letter discusses family news, home activities such as painting porch furniture and preparing for a bridge club lunch, and conveys affection and well-wishes. The envelope bears multiple postal markings, including air mail and return-to-sender stamps, and was ultimately unclaimed and returned to the sender.
